stories.headline: Today marks the 78th anniversary of the Nakba

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: To the Nakba glorifiers: The Nakba was the result of the Arab rejection of the UN Partition Plan & decision to launch war to annihilate the State of Israel. You can't start war, openly vow to “throw the Jews into the sea,” and then pretend being the victim of the conflict you started. The real forgotten victims are the 850,000 Jewish refugees expelled from Arab countries at the same time.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Today marks the 78th anniversary of the Nakba (catastrophe), during which more than 750,000 Palestinians were expelled and over 400 villages were destroyed to establish the State of Israel.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: I’m a 4th generation Palestinian, and the Nakba has never ended. They’ve made sure that every new generation of Palestinians watches a live-streamed genocide in real time, just to keep their pedophilic little state alive.

stories.headline: Matthew McConaughey shares story of living in Peru to reconnect with his identity.
stories.body: Matthew McConaughey once exiled himself from Hollywood and lived as Mateo in Peru for 22 days without electricity. “I needed to get my feet on the ground. So I click out. Boom. Go to Peru. I needed to find it, to check the validation. I knew I had it, I just had to go prove it again. But I did question, now that I just got famous, I’ve got all this affiliation for this and that and the other, and I’m trying to decipher which part’s real, which part’s bullsh*t...  I needed to meet people who knew me as Mateo. And at the end of 22 days, the tears in their eyes and the tears in my eyes and the hugs we had on the sadness and happiness of saying goodbye were all based off of the man they met named Mateo, who had nothing to do with the celebrity. It reaffirmed my own identity that, ‘Oh, I still got it. This is based on me.’” (via "No Magic Pill" podcast)

stories.headline: New research links optimism to better heart health
stories.body:
For decades, heart health advice has focused on a few key pillars: eat healthy, exercise and manage blood pressure and stress.

But new research suggests there may be a less obvious factor that can influence cardiovascular health: optimism. https://www.washingtonpost.com/lifestyle/2026/05/14/studies-link-optimism-better-heart-health-lower-risk-disease/?utm_campaign=wp_main&utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social

stories.headline: Removing one protein halts Alzheimer's symptoms in studies
stories.body:
🚨 THEY REMOVED ONE PROTEIN… AND ALZHEIMER’S SYMPTOMS STOPPED

Scientists made a shocking discovery in Alzheimer’s research. Even when harmful brain plaques were still present, removing just one protein stopped memory and brain function problems in studies. This suggests the real trigger behind Alzheimer’s may not be the plaques alone, but hidden processes happening deep inside brain cells.

The breakthrough could completely change how future Alzheimer’s treatments are designed — giving new hope to millions of families worldwide.

Source:
University of California San Diego. Removing one protein halts Alzheimer’s symptoms despite remaining plaques
